task:
fix: paginate() returns an empty final page when total is an exact multiple of page_size.

```json
{"acceptance_criteria": ["paginate() never yields an empty trailing page", "All pagination tests pass"]}
```
acceptance_criteria: paginate() never yields an empty trailing page

test_evidence.test_output:
FAILED tests/test_pagination.py::test_exact_multiple - AssertionError: got empty page
FAILED tests/test_pagination.py::test_last_page_len - IndexError
===== 2 failed, 39 passed in 2.02s =====

diff:
diff --git a/core/pagination.py b/core/pagination.py
index 5f6a7b8..9c0d1e2 100644
--- a/core/pagination.py
+++ b/core/pagination.py
@@ -4,8 +4,8 @@ def paginate(items, page_size):
     pages = []
-    for i in range(0, len(items) + 1, page_size):
+    for i in range(0, max(len(items), 1), page_size):
         pages.append(items[i:i + page_size])
     return pages
diff --git a/tests/test_pagination.py b/tests/test_pagination.py
index 8b9c0d1..2e3f4a5 100644
--- a/tests/test_pagination.py
+++ b/tests/test_pagination.py
@@ -15,3 +15,8 @@ def test_basic_split():
     assert len(paginate(list(range(10)), 3)) == 4
+
+
+def test_exact_multiple():
+    assert paginate(list(range(9)), 3) == [[0, 1, 2], [3, 4, 5], [6, 7, 8]]
rationale: Evidence-grounded rejection: the threaded test results show the suite FAILED — including the very test added for this fix — and an explicit criterion says all pagination tests pass. A grader ignoring the evidence block and judging the diff text alone false-approves. (In production failing tests never reach the oracle; this probes evidence weighting directly.)
